items.title: Trump Says He 'Couldn't Care Less' After Iran Abandons U.S. Memorandum
items.desc: U.S. President Donald Trump dismissed Iran's withdrawal from the U.S. memorandum while mourning two troops killed in Jordan as Washington expanded strikes on Iranian targets.

items.title: Iran Says It Struck Two U.S. Bases in Kuwait as Conflict Spreads Across Gulf
items.desc: Tehran claims drone attacks targeted American military facilities in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan after fresh U.S. airstrikes, while fears of a wider regional war grow

items.title: US Launches Fresh Strikes Against Iran at 6 p.m. Friday, Retaliating for Jordan Deaths and Hormuz attacks

items.title: U.S. State Department Issues Worldwide Security Alert Amid Rising Middle East Tensions
items.desc: Americans urged to exercise increased caution as Washington warns of potential escalation, travel disruptions, and threats to U.S. interests abroad

items.title: KRG to Launch Taxi Meter Pilot Program in Erbil and Sulaimani
items.desc: Transport Ministry says initiative aims to standardize fares and protect passengers through a fair pricing system

items.title: Eighth night of US-Iran strikes hits military targets
items.desc: Shafaq News- Washington The United States and Iran exchanged strikes for an eighth consecutive night, with Washington targeting Iranian military infrastructure and Tehran claiming retaliatory drone attacks on US military installations in Kuwait. US Central Command (CENTCOM) said on Sunday that American forces struck coastal surveillance systems, air defense positions, maritime capabilities, missil

items.title: England fire past France in 10-goal bronze-final
items.desc: Shafaq News- Miami England secured their first World Cup third-place finish after beating France 6-4 in a record-breaking contest in Miami, with Bukayo Saka scoring a hat-trick. Thomas Tuchel’s side produced a four-goal first half as Declan Rice opened the scoring in the third minute, Ezri Konsa headed in from a corner, and Saka struck twice before the interval. France responded immediately

items.title: Drone wreckage sparks fires near Akre in Duhok
items.desc: Shafaq News- Duhok Debris from a drone fell near the village of Kand Kali in the Akre district of Duhok province on Sunday, setting fire to farmland and grass-covered ground, according to a local security source and eyewitnesses who spoke to Shafaq News. The aircraft came down close to the village before flames broke out in several agricultural plots and surrounding grassland, and the fire spread

items.title: Drone hits Kurdish opposition site west of Erbil
items.desc: Shafaq News- Erbil A drone attack struck a headquarters of the Kurdistan National Army on Sunday evening in the Chamshar area west of Erbil province, according to a local source. The targeted site sits close to the Darashakran camp, with no immediate information available on casualties or the scale of material damage. The Kurdistan National Army is the armed wing of the Kurdistan Freedom Party (PA

items.title: US intercepts five commercial vessels in Hormuz
items.desc: Shafaq News- Washington US forces have redirected five commercial vessels and disabled one ship since July 18, US Central Command (CENTCOM) said on Saturday. CENTCOM noted that Washington continues to “strictly enforce the ongoing naval blockade against Iran.” USS Donald Cook (DDG 75) transits the Arabian Sea while an MH-60S Sea Hawk flies nearby. items.title: Andrew Tate and brother arrested in US after more charges laid against them in UK
items.desc: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are "innocent".

items.title: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
items.desc: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.

items.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
items.desc: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

items.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
items.desc: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

items.title: What this desert city can teach the world about tackling heat deaths
items.desc: Maricopa County in Arizona has some of the hottest temperatures, but it has reduced the number of heat-related deaths.

items.title: Josh Kerr of Britain breaks 27-year-old world record in the mile
items.desc: Kerr announced his bid to break the world record months in advance, and pulled off the plan in London on Saturday.

items.title: U.S. launches new airstrikes to 'punish' Iran for troop deaths
items.desc: Two U.S. service members were killed in action while "partner forces defended against Iranian ballistic missile and drone attacks," U.S. Central Command said Saturday.

items.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
items.desc: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.

items.title: 8 killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
items.desc: Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.

items.title: The U.S. and Iran blow past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
items.desc: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

items.title: US and Iran exchange strikes after two US deaths in Jordan attack
items.desc: US forces target Iranian sites for the eighth night in a row, while Iran says it fired drones at the US military in Kuwait.

items.title: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
items.desc: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.

items.title: US strikes oil tanker with missiles as it enforces new Iran blockade
items.desc: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'
items.desc: US President Donald Trump says he is yet to decide whether or not he will "finish off" Iran.

items.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
items.desc: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.
